note: These FAQs are lifted/condensed from the legacy site. Use them verbatim-ish for FAQPage schema AND visible accordions. Answer-first phrasing is intentional for AI/answer-engine extraction. Add localized variants on city pages.

general.a: Text us a few clear photos of the items you want removed. We review them and send a straightforward quote, usually very quickly — the fastest way to get pricing without waiting for an in-person visit.

general.q: How accurate is a quote based on photos?
general.a: Photo quotes work well for most jobs. If anything changes once we arrive, we walk through it with you before starting, so pricing stays clear and upfront.

general.q: What size dump trailer do I need?
general.a: Most customers aren't sure at first, and that's okay. We ask a few quick questions about your project and help you choose the right trailer size so you're not overpaying or running out of space.

general.q: Is your equipment safe for driveways?
general.a: Yes. Our dump trailers are designed to be driveway-safe and placed carefully to avoid damage. If you have concerns about placement, let us know before delivery.

We handle a wide range of household, yard, and renovation items — furniture, mattresses, appliances, electronics, yard debris, bulk items, and construction/renovation debris. If it's taking up space, there's a good chance we can help. When in doubt, send a photo.

general.q: How soon can you come out or deliver a trailer?
general.a: In many cases we can schedule quickly, including same-day or next-day availability. Response time depends on the job and current schedule, but we always aim to move fast and communicate clearly.

whatWeTake.q: Can you remove items from inside my home or business?
whatWeTake.a: Yes. We remove items from wherever they're located — garages, spare rooms, offices, storage units, and similar spaces — not just the curb. Photos ahead of time help us plan access.

whatWeTake.q: Do you remove both residential and commercial items?
whatWeTake.a: Yes. We handle household items, office furniture, shelving and storage, retail fixtures, and back-of-house materials. A quick description or photo usually clears up any questions.

whatWeTake.q: Can you take renovation and construction debris?
whatWeTake.a: Yes. We handle drywall, flooring, cabinets, scrap wood, and leftover fixtures after remodels, repairs, or cleanouts. A photo helps us confirm volume and any special handling.

whatWeTake.q: Do you take large, awkward items like hot tubs or sheds?
whatWeTake.a: Yes. We handle bulky removals and light-demolition items like hot tubs and sheds. These usually need extra planning for size or access, so photos are the fastest way to confirm.

whatWeTake.q: What if I have a mixed pile of different items?
whatWeTake.a: Very common during cleanouts and renovations. Mixed loads are quoted by overall volume and material type. A few photos from different angles give us enough context for a clear quote.

whatWeTake.q: Are there items you don't accept?
whatWeTake.a: For safety and disposal reasons we do not take hazardous or restricted materials — chemicals, paints, propane tanks, pool chemicals, used oil, batteries, pesticides, asbestos, or biohazard waste. Some items (like bed bug–infested furniture) need special handling, always explained before work begins.

pricing.q: How much does junk removal cost?
pricing.a: Junk removal is priced by how much space your items fill in our 18-yard dump trailer, including labor and disposal: minimum load $99, quarter load $289, half load $479, three-quarter load $659, and a full load $795. Send a few photos and we'll confirm which tier fits.

pricing.q: How much does a dumpster rental cost?
pricing.a: Our 18-yard dump trailer rents for $300 for 1 day or $350 for 2–3 days. Our 21-yard trailer is $400 for 1 day or $450 for 2–3 days. The base price is plus dump fees at $140 per ton. A $50 daily surcharge applies after day 3.

pricing.q: How is junk removal pricing calculated?
pricing.a: Pricing is based on how much space your items take up in our 18-yard trailer, not the number of individual items. The price includes labor and disposal, so you'll know the total before anything is removed.

pricing.q: What's included in the price I'm quoted?
pricing.a: Your quote covers the full removal: labor for loading, transportation to approved disposal or recycling facilities, dump/processing fees, and time on-site. Anything requiring extra handling is discussed ahead of time.

pricing.q: Why can prices vary from one job to another?
pricing.a: Two piles that look similar can take up very different amounts of space once loaded. Weight, density, access, and preparation all affect pricing, which is why photos or an on-site look help.

pricing.q: Are there situations where additional charges may apply?
pricing.a: Yes — items that need bagging or breaking down, heavy or oversized items needing extra labor, and bed bug–infested furniture. Nothing is added without a clear explanation and your approval.
Yes. Discounts are available for military members, veterans, seniors, and first responders. Mention it when requesting your quote so it can be applied upfront.

pricing.q: What's the fastest way to get an accurate price?
pricing.a: Sending photos from a few angles is usually quickest. For more complex jobs, an in-person estimate can also be scheduled.

about.q: What makes Sunshine State Junk Removal different?
about.a: Being family-owned and veteran-owned shapes how every job is approached — clear communication from first message to final pickup, respect for your time and property, accountability on every job, and a hands-on approach instead of rotating crews.

about.q: Why do you offer photo-based quotes?
about.a: Photo quotes let us respond faster and give a clear starting price without unnecessary delays.

about.q: Do you handle both small pickups and larger projects?
about.a: Yes — everything from single-item removals to larger cleanouts and ongoing projects, each with the same planning and attention.

about.q: What happens to the items after they're removed?
about.a: Items are sorted whenever possible. Scrap metal, appliances, and electronics are recycled when appropriate, and usable items may be donated to reduce landfill waste.

about.q: Who will I be working with during my project?
about.a: A local, family-run team led by Cesar and his wife Lynzee, for clear communication and consistent service start to finish.
